Funda Calapverdi [...] DualStream is one studio that streams to your Desktop and Mobile audiences at the same time - same sources, arranged for each scene, live everywhere in one click. The DualStream Relay - one upload from your PC, live on every platform from our servers. And if your connection drops mid-broadcast, Relay keeps every platform live on your standby screen and picks back up the moment you reconnect. Native Alerts, Per-Source audio control, VTuber support, instant replay clips (horizontal and vertical), cinema grade effects, pop-out multi-platform chat and so much more is all built in.

results.title: How do i switch from pc stream to mobile stream without ending stream
results.content:
## Top Comments
- IRL streams switching from phone to desktop or desktop to phone are very common. The simplest method has been mentioned, using twitch's built-in "disconnect protection". The next simplest method puts some polish on the process and offers a nicer viewer experience: Look into a $10/month Belabox Cloud  relay combined with IRL Pro (for Android)  or Moblin (for iPhone)  . Using this setup you'll send your phone video to the relay and OBS will pull the video from the relay and send it off to twitch just as if you're sitting at the desktop. Combined with tools like NOALBS  for automatic scene switching and obs-web  for remote control, you're all set for an easy and fun IRL stream [...] - VDO.Ninja is an option you can use where you change OBS scenes to a scene using VDO.Ninja. Your phone is in the same "room" as your OBS so it connects together. The gold standard would be using an android app called IRL Pro, but you need to know how to forward ports on your router to get it working for a seamless transition from gaming to IRL stream. [...] - Best way I worked out is to first make sure you have "Disconnect Protection" turned on through your Creator Dashboard on Twitch. Creator Dashboard -> Settings -> Stream -> Disconnection Protection. Then, when you are ready to switch, you need to kill OBS through Task Manager, then the disconnect will kick in for 90 seconds. Start up on your mobile, and voila! No drop. You do only have 90 seconds before it then says offline and your uptime starts again and everyone has to refresh. Do the reverse when going mobile to PC, you have to forcefully kill the app, tricking Twitch into thinking it has disconnected. Then start up your stream on OBS on your PC. Hope this helps. DM if you need more explanation for help! Good luck out there!

Luckily, this is super simple because all three of these will share the same
settings. Just go to settings in the bottom right corner and then click on
output. Then, change the output mode from simple to advanced. If you're on an
Nvidia graphics card, you're going to choose Nvidia NVENC H.264. If you have
an AMD graphics card, you'll use AMD AMF H.264. And if you don't have either of
those, then you'll use X.264, but you might lag a little bit and you should

results.title: What tool is best for streaming to multiple platforms ...
results.content:
- restreamer, it's free
- None
- Restream is free if you want to stream only to 2 platforms, and if you're using OBS you also have no quality supresión or watermark. Works just great, 100% recommend it. If you have a super good internet and computer (not my case) you can use the multi rtpm obs plugin that is also free, but consumes a little bit more of your computer. There's a third option which is using streamlabs premium (or whatever the name it is) which is cheaper than the paid restream plan but I'm really not a fan of streamlabs. Also the other options are free.
- i started using restream. pretty simple to set up and has a window to watch all chats at same time.
